entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: A staple food crop (Sorghum bicolor L. Moench) evaluated for grain yield stability in Niger.

entities.name: NIGER
entities.description: A country in West Africa where sorghum is a staple food crop and the study was conducted.

entities.name: SORGHUM MIDGE
entities.description: A pest that constrains the production of sorghum.

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The amount of grain produced per unit area, used as a measure of productivity in sorghum.

entities.name: ALPHA (0.1) LATTICE
entities.description: The experimental design used for evaluating 282 sorghum genotypes with two replications.

entities.name: GGE BIPLOT ANALYSIS
entities.description: A statistical method used to study grain yield stability and genotype by environment interactions.

entities.name: G X E INTERACTIONS
entities.description: The interaction between genotype and environment that affects the expression of traits like grain yield.

entities.name: STABILITY
entities.description: The consistency of a genotype's performance across different environments.

entities.name: KONNI
entities.description: A specific location in Niger used as an environment for evaluating sorghum genotypes.

entities.name: MARADI
entities.description: A specific location in Niger used as an environment for evaluating sorghum genotypes.

entities.name: L232
entities.description: A specific sorghum genotype identified as the best for grain yield in Konni and Maradi.

entities.name: L17
entities.description: A specific sorghum genotype identified as the best for grain yield in the second planting date at Konni.
